notes: Pioneer in SMP and NUMA server architecture; acquired by IBM in 1999 for approx. $810M.
Technology analyst firm founded in Boston; acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2007, later became part of Spiceworks Ziff Davis.
Referenced for ES/9000 Sysplex clustering and SP2 parallel systems; acquired Sequent in 1999.
Sequent's NUMA-Q was built on Intel microprocessors; Intel's x86 platform was central to the enterprise superserver thesis.
Referenced for VAXcluster technology; acquired by Compaq in 1998, which was later acquired by HP in 2002.
Referenced for HP 9000 SMP systems; split into HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise in 2015.
Referenced for Oracle Parallel Server as an example of cluster-aware software complexity.
